yields.Yxs_units: mg cell dry weight / mg phenol

growth_records.cite: Szilveszter, Szabolcs et al. Kinetic characterization of a new phenol degrading Acinetobacter towneri strain isolated from landfill leachate treating bioreactor. World Journal of Microbiology & Biotechnology (2023). DOI:10.1007/s11274-022-03487-y. [PMC9842574]
growth_records.snippet: 100 | 2 | 160 | 25.0 | 0.12 | 0.6 (Table 2: initial phenol mg/L | lag h | max biomass mg/L | phenol biodegradation rate mg/L/h | specific growth rate 1/h | growth yield mg cell/mg phenol)
growth_records.notes: Batch flask, MP medium + 100 mg/L phenol as sole carbon source, 28 C, 150 rpm, aerobic (orbital shaker). From Table 2 (experimental): mu=0.12 1/h, volumetric phenol biodegradation rate=25.0 mg/L/h, growth yield Y=0.6 mg cell/mg phenol, max biomass=160 mg/L, lag=2 h. doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.12 = 5.78 h. Specific phenol uptake qS = mu/Y = 0.12/0.6 = 0.2000 g phenol/gDW/h; /94.11 g/mol *1000 = 2.125 mmol/gDW/h. mu reported by authors (Eq.1 mu=ln(X2/X1)/(t2-t1) from OD590). pH not explicitly restated for the biodegradation assay; enrichment steps were at pH 8; MP medium is phosphate-buffered. (Table 1 screening 'average phenol consumption rate' C_phenol/Δt for CFII-87 at 100 mg/L = 25.0 mg/L/h, slightly different from Table 2 detailed value.)

up.method: average consumption = C_phenol/Δt (time to complete phenol depletion), Table 1

growth_records.cite: Szilveszter, Szabolcs et al. Kinetic characterization of a new phenol degrading Acinetobacter towneri strain isolated from landfill leachate treating bioreactor. World Journal of Microbiology & Biotechnology (2023). DOI:10.1007/s11274-022-03487-y. [PMC9842574]
growth_records.snippet: Acinetobacter towneri CFII-101 Average phenol consumption rate (mg/L/h): C_phenol=100 mg/L -> 16.67 (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 screening: average phenol consumption (volumetric uptake) rate = C_phenol/Δt = 100 mg/L / time-to-complete-depletion = 16.67 mg/L/h, experimentally measured. No specific growth rate/biomass/yield reported for this strain at this condition, so qS(specific) not derivable. Species-level ID only for A. towneri strains (CFII-101) and Arthrobacter crystallopoietes (CFII-104, CFII-112); others reported as Acinetobacter sp. Same MP medium, 28 C, 150 rpm, pH not restated for assay (enrichment pH 8).
